use std::sync::OnceLock;

struct AppConfig {
    db_pool: Vec<String>,
    templates: Vec<String>,
    feature_flags: Vec<String>,
}

fn load_config() -> AppConfig {
    std::thread::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_secs(2));
    AppConfig {
        db_pool: (0..100).map(|i| format!("conn_{}", i)).collect(),
        templates: (0..50).map(|i| format!("template_{}", i)).collect(),
        feature_flags: vec!["flag_a".into(), "flag_b".into()],
    }
}

static CONFIG: OnceLock<AppConfig> = OnceLock::new();

// GOOD: explicit warmup before serving traffic
async fn warmup() {
    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(|| {
        CONFIG.get_or_init(|| load_config());
    }).await.unwrap();
}

async fn handle_request() -> &'static AppConfig {
    // by now config is guaranteed initialized
    CONFIG.get().expect("warmup must run first")
}
